replication_study.hypothesis: Among preschool teachers, weekend relaxation at Time 2 (Sunday night) positively predicts joviality at Time 3 (following Friday) when controlling for joviality at Time 1 (previous Friday), other weekend recovery experiences (detachment, mastery, control), non-work hassles, and demographics (age, gender, children, work hours, work days).

data_plan.dataset_identifier: K17 preschool teachers weekend recovery dataset (replication_data/k17_processed_data.csv), 2021 cohort
data_plan.source_type: Longitudinal surveys/questionnaires (Recovery Experience Questionnaire, PANAS-X, non-work hassles checklist)
data_plan.wave_or_subset: Three time points: Time 1 (Friday after work), Time 2 (Sunday night), Time 3 (following Friday after work); see k17_data_dictionary.csv and Data_Code_Book.csv
data_plan.sample_size: 339 participants in processed dataset; effective N for focal regression computed at runtime by analysis script (listwise deletion)
data_plan.access_details: De-identified CSV files provided locally in /app/data/replication_data; identifiers hashed (see Data_Code_Book.csv).

qualification.explanation: This dataset is a new, independent sample of preschool teachers measured at the same three time points with the same instruments (REQ, PANAS-X) and the same modeling strategy as the original. It provides a different sample collected under similar conditions, allowing a robust test of the focal association.
qualification.similarity_to_original: The original study measured weekend recovery experiences (relaxation, mastery, control, detachment), non-work hassles, and affect (PANAS-X) at T1/T2/T3, and tested the effect of relaxation on joviality controlling for T1 joviality and covariates (post_registration.json; original_paper.pdf summary). The replication dataset contains the same constructs and timing: REQ items (req_relax_1..4, req_mastery_1..4, req_control_1..4, req_detach_1..4), non-work hassles items (has_1..has_53), and PANAS-X items for joviality aggregated at T1 and T3 to form T1_panas_joviality and T3_panas_joviality (replication_data/k17_data_dictionary.csv; Data_Code_Book.csv; k17_data_prep.R logic).
qualification.deviation_from_original: The replication sample appears to be collected in 2021 (age computed as 2021 - birthyear; see k17_data_prep.R) and is larger (processed N=339) than the original 229 German preschool teachers (post_registration.json). The replication data also include some additional variables not modeled in the focal test (e.g., uplifts; see Data_Code_Book.csv). While the original sample was German preschool teachers (post_registration.json), the replication metadata do not explicitly state the country; however, the same occupational group and instrument structure are maintained.

data_plan.notes: Variables are coded as numeric: gender 1=male, 2=female (k17_data_dictionary.csv). Children is recoded to 0/1 (k17_data_prep.R). PANAS-X and REQ indices are computed as row means/sums per R script (k17_data_prep.R); Python rewrite mirrors this. Paths during execution must reference /app/data. The processed dataset already exists, but the Python data prep script can regenerate it from /app/data/replication_data/Raw data. The analysis fits both unstandardized and standardized models; standardized matches the R scale() usage. All IO is confined to /app/data.

steps: Ensure raw data are available under /app/data/replication_data/Raw data (k17_t1_data.csv, k17_t2_data.csv, k17_t3_data.csv).

Option A (recommended): Run Python data preparation to reproduce processed dataset: python /app/data/replication_data/k17_data_prep__py.py, which saves /app/data/replication_data/k17_processed_data.csv.
Option B: Use the provided processed dataset directly if present at /app/data/replication_data/k17_processed_data.csv.
Review output written to /app/data/k17_results.json for focal coefficient and p-value.
planned_method.models: Hierarchical OLS regression predicting T3 joviality. Focal model includes controls (age, gender, children, work_hours, work_days), baseline T1 joviality, weekend experiences (req_control, req_mastery, req_relax, req_detach), and hassles. Estimates reported for both unstandardized and standardized (z-score) specifications.
planned_method.outcome_variable: T3_panas_joviality (mean of PANAS-X joviality items at Time 3)
planned_method.independent_variables: Primary predictor: req_relax (mean of REQ relaxation items at Time 2). Additional predictors for the focal model: T1_panas_joviality (baseline), req_control, req_mastery, req_detach, hassles.

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Coefficient on req_relax in the full model (effect of weekend relaxation on T3 joviality, controlling for covariates).
planned_estimation_and_test.test: t-test on the req_relax coefficient. Primary inference is one-tailed (H1: beta > 0) at alpha=0.05; statsmodels p-values are two-tailed, so success corresponds to two-tailed p < 0.10 with positive sign.

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Listwise deletion (default behavior of OLS with missing='drop' in the Python code; equivalent to R's na.action=na.omit).
planned_method.multiple_testing_policy: Single focal test; no multiple-testing correction planned. Exploratory outcomes, if any, should be clearly labeled and not used for focal inference.
planned_method.inference_criteria: Successful replication requires a positive coefficient on req_relax with one-tailed p < 0.05 (equivalently, two-tailed p < 0.10) in the full model. Magnitude comparable to original standardized beta ≈ 0.15 (initial_details.txt; post_registration.json) will be reported but not required for success.

files.replication_data/k17_data_prep__py.py: Python rewrite of k17_data_prep.R. Reads raw survey files from /app/data/replication_data/Raw data, filters participants present in all waves and finished, maps item columns to named variables, constructs indices (REQ, hassles, PANAS composites at T1 and T3), computes age, merges T1/T2/T3, deduplicates by RecipientEmail, and saves /app/data/replication_data/k17_processed_data.csv.
files.replication_data/k17_analysis__py.py: Python rewrite of the focal hierarchical regression from k17_analysis.R. Loads /app/data/replication_data/k17_processed_data.csv (with a fallback to /app/data/k17_processed_data.csv), fits OLS models (unstandardized and standardized), and outputs /app/data/k17_results.json with R^2, N, and focal coefficient/p-value for req_relax.

codebase.notes: All read/write operations occur under /app/data to ensure containerized execution. The analysis code depends on statsmodels for OLS, with pandas/numpy for data handling. Run the data prep prior to analysis if you need to regenerate the processed dataset from raw files.

instructions: Compare sign and significance to the original focal result (β ≈ 0.15, p < 0.01; initial_details.txt, post_registration.json).
analysis.comparison_metrics: Directionality (positive), significance (one-tailed alpha=0.05; equivalently two-tailed p < 0.10) for req_relax in the full model; standardized effect magnitude compared to original β ≈ 0.15; report R^2 change descriptively if needed.
